'''Paul Vaughan''' (born 23 April 1991) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} forward for the [[York Knights]] in the [[Super League]]. He has played for both [[Italy national rugby league team|Italy]] and [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] at international level.

He previously played for the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]], [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] and [[Canberra Raiders]] in the [[National Rugby League]] and [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[English Super League]], and at representative level for [[Country New South Wales rugby league team|NSW Country]], [[NRL All Stars team|NRL All Stars]] and [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] in the [[State of Origin series]].

==Background==
[[File:Paul Vaughan cropped.jpg|thumb|right|Vaughan playing for the Raiders in 2011]]
Vaughan was born in [[Canberra]], [[Australian Capital Territory]], and is of [[Italians|Italian]] descent through his maternal grandmother.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Buckley |first=James |date=2017-09-15 |title=Dragons prop Paul Vaughan set for second World Cup with Italy ... or Australia |work=The Sydney Morning Herald  |url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/dragons-prop-paul-vaughan-set-for-second-world-cup-with-italy--or-australia-20170914-gyhijc.html |url-status=live |url-access=limited |access-date=2017-09-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180729011010/https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/dragons-prop-paul-vaughan-set-for-second-world-cup-with-italy--or-australia-20170914-gyhijc.html |archive-date=2018-07-29}}</ref>

He played his junior rugby league for the [[Gungahlin Bulls]] before being signed by the [[Canberra Raiders]], with whom he played in the [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|National Youth Competition]] in 2010 and 2011.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Paul Vaughan – Player |url=http://rleague.com/players/Paul-Vaughan_2914/Matches/ |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130907054900/http://rleague.com/players/Paul-Vaughan_2914/Matches/ |archive-date=2013-09-07 |access-date=2013-09-27 |website=rleague.com}}</ref> In 2012, Vaughan graduated to the Raiders' [[NSW Cup]] team, the [[Mount Pritchard Mounties]], winning the Mounties' Player's Player award and being named on the interchange bench in the 2012 [[NSW Cup]] Team of the Year.

==Playing career==
===2013===
On 18 April, Vaughan re-signed with the Raiders on a 2-year contract to the end of the 2015 season.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2013-04-18 |title=Raiders re-sign McIlwrick and Vaughan |url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/raiders-re-sign-mcilwrick-and-vaughan-9161/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160320104233/https://www.zerotackle.com/raiders-re-sign-mcilwrick-and-vaughan-9161/ |archive-date=2016-03-20 |access-date=2013-04-27 |website=Zero Tackle}}</ref> In Round 7 of the [[2013 NRL season]], Vaughan made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for the Canberra Raiders off the interchange bench against the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] in the Raiders 30–12 loss at [[1300SMILES Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite news |last=Badel |first=Peter |date=2013-04-28 |title=Matt Bowen stars as North Queensland Cowboys beat Canberra Raiders 30-12 |work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/matt-bowen-stars-as-north-queensland-cowboys-beat-canberra-raiders-30-12/news-story/e0c7caaa7e3defd70357934ad7297c4c |url-status=live |access-date=2015-10-31 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201111033716/https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/matt-bowen-stars-as-north-queensland-cowboys-beat-canberra-raiders-30-12/news-story/e0c7caaa7e3defd70357934ad7297c4c?nk=cb2285501a43f58f69e436827d66fd2b-1605065836 |archive-date=2020-11-11}}</ref>{{failed verification |date=April 2020 |reason=Vaughan is not mentioned in this article}} In Round 14 against the [[Penrith Panthers]], Vaughan scored his first NRL career try in the Raiders 24–12 win at [[Canberra Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite news |last=Tuxworth |first=Jon |date=2013-06-15 |title=Raiders surge past Panthers |work=The Sydney Morning Herald  |url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/raiders-surge-past-panthers-20130615-2obgf.html |url-status=live |url-access=limited |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180729013606/https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/raiders-surge-past-panthers-20130615-2obgf.html |archive-date=2018-07-29}}</ref> Vaughan finished the [[2013 NRL season]] being named as the Raiders Rookie of the Year, playing in 18 matches and scoring a try in his debut year in the NRL.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Tuxworth |first=Jon |date=2013-12-08 |title=Canberra Raiders rookie Paul Vaughan likes the hue of NSW blue |work=The Sydney Morning Herald  |url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/canberra-raiders-rookie-paul-vaughan-likes-the-hue-of-nsw-blue-20131207-2yydl.html |url-status=live |url-access=limited |access-date=2015-03-31 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180729015347/https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/canberra-raiders-rookie-paul-vaughan-likes-the-hue-of-nsw-blue-20131207-2yydl.html |archive-date=2018-07-29}}</ref> In October, Vaughan was named in the [[Italy national rugby league team|Italy]] 24-man squad for the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup|2013 World Cup]],<ref>{{Cite web |date=2013-09-24 |title=Italy name squad for RLWC2013 |url=http://www.rlwc2013.com/rugby-league-world-news/article/1117/italy-name-squad-for-rlwc |url-status=usurped |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130927082325/http://www.rlwc2013.com/rugby-league-world-news/article/1117/italy-name-squad-for-rlwc |archive-date=2013-09-27 |website=Rugby League World Cup 2013 |publisher=Rugby League International Federation}}</ref> playing in all of their three matches.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Paul Vaughan – World Cup matches |url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTE5LS03LS0tLS0tMjAyODctLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t |access-date=2018-07-29 |publisher=Rugby League Project}}</ref>

===2014===
In Round 7 against the [[Melbourne Storm]], Vaughan scored a solo effort try in the 79th minute to win the match for the Raiders 24–22 at Canberra Stadium.<ref>{{Cite news |date=2014-04-20 |title=Last-gasp payback as Storm falls on late try |work=The Australian |agency=Australian Associated Press |url=https://www.theaustralian.com.au/sport/nrl/lastgasp-payback-as-storm-falls-on-late-try/news-story/99d183924fe3f5a061d67bf815f208da |url-status=live |url-access=subscription |access-date=2015-10-31 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180729081704/https://www.theaustralian.com.au/sport/nrl/lastgasp-payback-as-storm-falls-on-late-try/news-story/99d183924fe3f5a061d67bf815f208da |archive-date=2018-07-29}}</ref> Vaughan earned selection in the [[Country New South Wales rugby league team|NSW Country]] team in the [[City vs Country Origin]] match off the interchange, scoring a try in the 26-all draw in [[Dubbo, New South Wales|Dubbo]].<ref>{{Cite news |last=Toohey |first=Barry |date=2014-05-04 |title=Hulking centre Jamal Idris stars in thrilling City-Country draw in Dubbo |work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/hulking-centre-jamal-idris-stars-in-thrilling-citycountry-draw-in-dubbo/story-fni3fbgz-1226904860045 |url-status=dead |access-date=2015-10-31 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140508102206/http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/hulking-centre-jamal-idris-stars-in-thrilling-citycountry-draw-in-dubbo/story-fni3fbgz-1226904860045 |archive-date=8 May 2014 }}</ref>{{Failed verification|date=July 2018}} Vaughan finished the [[2014 NRL season]] with him playing in 23 matches and scoring 7 tries for the Raiders. On 10 September, Vaughan re-signed with the Raiders until the end of the 2017 season.<ref>{{Cite news |date=2014-09-10 |title=Paul Vaughan re-signs with Canberra Raiders until end of 2017 |work=The Sydney Morning Herald  |url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/paul-vaughan-resigns-with-canberra-raiders-until-end-of-2017-20140910-10f71d.html |url-status=live |access-date=2015-03-31 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180729024543/https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/paul-vaughan-resigns-with-canberra-raiders-until-end-of-2017-20140910-10f71d.html |archive-date=2018-07-29}}</ref>

===2015===
On 3 May, Vaughan played for New South Wales Country against [[City New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales City]] in the 2015 City vs Country Origin match, playing off the interchange bench in Country's 34–22 win at [[Wagga Wagga]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Brady |first=Jack |date=2015-05-03 |title=Country overcome City in seesawing affair |url=http://www.nrl.com/country-overcome-city-in-seesawing-affair/tabid/10874/newsid/85906/default.aspx |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180626164416/https://www.nrl.com/news/2015/05/03/country-overcome-city-in-seesawing-affair/ |archive-date=2018-06-26 |publisher=National Rugby League}}</ref> In Round 20 against the Penrith Panthers, he scored his first career double in the Raiders' 34–24 win at [[Penrith Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2015-07-26 |title=Raiders outlast Panthers in high-scoring clash |url=http://www.nrl.com/raiders-outlast-panthers-in-high-scoring-clash/tabid/10874/newsid/88372/default.aspx |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180729025721/https://www.nrl.com/news/2015/07/26/raiders-outlast-panthers-in-high-scoring-clash/ |archive-date=2018-07-29 |publisher=National Rugby League}}</ref> He finished the [[2015 NRL season|2015 season]] having played in all of the Raiders' 24 matches and scoring 4 tries.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Custom Match List |url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/Mi0xNjYwLTQtLS0tLS0yMDI4Ny0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0= |publisher=Rugby League Project}}</ref> On 8 September, he was named in the 2015 [[Prime Minister's XIII]] squad<ref>{{Cite web |date=2015-09-08 |title=Prime Minister's XIII train-on squad |url=http://www.nrl.com/prime-ministers-xiii-train-on-squad/tabid/10874/newsid/89627/default.aspx |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180729030439/https://www.nrl.com/news/2015/09/08/prime-ministers-xiii-train-on-squad/ |archive-date=2018-07-29 |access-date=2015-09-16 |publisher=National Rugby League}}</ref> but later pulled out due to injury.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Gaskin |first=Lee |date=2015-09-13 |title=Kangaroos forward Josh Papalii one of four Raiders players ruled out of PM's XIII squad |work=The Canberra Times  |url=https://www.canberratimes.com.au/sport/nrl/kangaroos-forward-josh-papalii-one-of-four-raiders-players-ruled-out-of-pms-xiii-squad-20150911-gjkikh.html |url-status=live |access-date=2015-09-16 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180729030822/https://www.canberratimes.com.au/sport/nrl/kangaroos-forward-josh-papalii-one-of-four-raiders-players-ruled-out-of-pms-xiii-squad-20150911-gjkikh.html |archive-date=2018-07-29}}</ref>

===2016===
On 30 January, Vaughan was named in the Raiders' [[2016 NRL Auckland Nines]] squad.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2016-01-30 |title=Raiders name 2016 Nines squad |url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/raiders-name-2016-nines-squad/ |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160131060244/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/raiders-name-2016-nines-squad/ |archive-date=2016-01-31 |access-date=2016-01-31 |website=Rugby League Week }}</ref> On 8 May, for the third year in a row, Vaughan played for New South Wales Country against New South Wales City, where he played off the interchange bench in the 44–30 loss in [[Tamworth, New South Wales|Tamworth]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Webeck |first=Tony |date=2016-02-28 |title=Young City side stun Country |url=https://www.nrl.com/young-city-side-stun-country/tabid/10874/newsid/95694/default.aspx |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180626163701/https://www.nrl.com/news/2016/05/08/young-city-side-stun-country/ |archive-date=2018-06-26 |access-date=2016-05-10 |publisher=National Rugby League}}</ref> After Round 18, Vaughan was dropped to the Raiders NSW Cup team the Mount Pritchard Mounties after Coach Ricky Stuart opted to select the newly arrived prop [[Junior Paulo (rugby league, born 1993)|Junior Paulo]] from the [[Parramatta Eels]], rising youngster [[Joseph Tapine]] and late bloomer rookie [[Clay Priest]] to be part of the forwards rotation.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walsh |first=Dan |date=2016-08-18 |title=NRL 2016: Raiders CEO Don Furner admits Paul Vaughan, Shaun Fensom could be forced elsewhere |url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/raiders/nrl-2016-raiders-ceo-don-furner-admits-paul-vaughan-shaun-fensom-could-be-forced-elsewhere/news-story/273ebca201e8de02354372564f9b8016 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160821175756/http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/raiders/nrl-2016-raiders-ceo-don-furner-admits-paul-vaughan-shaun-fensom-could-be-forced-elsewhere/news-story/273ebca201e8de02354372564f9b8016 |archive-date=2016-08-21 |access-date=2016-12-05 |work=[[Fox Sports]]}}</ref> But after Clay Priest suffered a season ending ankle injury, Vaughan beat out overlooked veteran forwards [[Shaun Fensom]] and [[Jeff Lima (rugby league)|Jeff Lima]] to fill in the vacant spot on the interchange bench for the finals.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Dutton |first=Chris |date=2016-09-15 |title=NRL finals: Recalled Raiders trio ready to step up after Clay Priest suffers injury heartbreak |work=The Canberra Times  |url=https://www.canberratimes.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-finals-canberra-raiders-heartbreak-as-clay-priest-breaks-ankle-at-training-20160915-grgxau.html |url-access=limited |access-date=2016-12-05}}</ref> Vaughan finished the [[2016 NRL season]] playing 20 matches and scoring 4 tries for the Canberra Raiders. On 15 October, it was announced that Vaughan signed a 3-year contract with the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] worth $1.75m, starting in 2017.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Polkinghorne |first=David |date=2016-10-15 |title=Canberra Raiders prop Paul Vaughan accepts St George Dragons' offer |work=The Canberra Times  |url=https://www.canberratimes.com.au/sport/nrl/canberra-raiders-prop-paul-vaughan-accepts-st-george-dragons-offer-20161015-gs33nz.html |url-status=dead |access-date=2016-12-05 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180729033631/https://www.canberratimes.com.au/sport/nrl/canberra-raiders-prop-paul-vaughan-accepts-st-george-dragons-offer-20161015-gs33nz.html |archive-date=2018-07-29}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Glover |first=Ben |date=2016-10-21 |title=St George Illawarra Dragons confirm signing of former Canberra Raiders star Paul Vaghan |url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/st-george-illawarra-dragons-confirm-signing-of-former-canberra-raiders-star-paul-vaghan/news-story/3bb5ea89d4c15c26170fb6a8b3098dc3 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161024072820/https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/st-george-illawarra-dragons-confirm-signing-of-former-canberra-raiders-star-paul-vaghan/news-story/3bb5ea89d4c15c26170fb6a8b3098dc3 |archive-date=2016-10-24 |access-date=2016-12-05 |work=[[Fox Sports]]}}</ref>

===2017===
On 10 February, Vaughan played for the [[World All Stars]] against the [[Indigenous All Stars (rugby league)|Indigenous All Stars]] in the [[2017 All Stars match]], playing off the interchange bench in the 34–8 loss at [[Hunter Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite news |date=2017-02-06 |title=World All Stars team changes |publisher=National Rugby League |url=http://www.nrl.com/world-all-stars-team-changes/tabid/10874/newsid/103567/default.aspx |url-status=live |access-date=2018-07-29 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180729172534/https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/02/06/world-all-stars-team-changes/ |archive-date=2018-07-29}}</ref> In Round 1 of the [[2017 NRL season]], Vaughan made his club debut for the St George Illawarra Dragons against the [[Penrith Panthers]], starting at prop in the 42–10 win at [[Jubilee Oval]].<ref>{{Cite news |title=Dragons slay Panthers in Kogarah boilover |work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/dragons/st-george-illawarra-dragons-defeat-penrith-panthers-4210-in-round-one-boilover/news-story/52510a54f2034112b7a22cbb8734fc7f |url-access=subscription}}</ref> In his next match, in Round 2 against the [[Parramatta Eels]], Vaughan scored his first club try for the Dragons in the 34–16 loss at [[WIN Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Kennedy |first=Chris |date=2017-03-12 |title=Semi scores four as Eels down Dragons |url=http://www.nrl.com/semi-scores-four-as-eels-down-dragons/tabid/10874/newsid/104551/default.aspx |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170312141856/http://www.nrl.com/semi-scores-four-as-eels-down-dragons/tabid/10874/newsid/104551/default.aspx |archive-date=2017-03-12 |access-date=2018-07-29 |publisher=National Rugby League}}</ref> On 13 September, Vaughan was named in the 2017 Prime Minister's XIII squad.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2017-09-13 |title=Mal Meninga names Australian Prime Minister's XIII for Papua New Guinea tour match |url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/mal-meninga-names-australian-prime-ministers-xiii-for-papua-new-guinea-tour-match/news-story/1b9ccca3eea6bd5f927656101c37e1c9 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170912215626/https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/mal-meninga-names-australian-prime-ministers-xiii-for-papua-new-guinea-tour-match/news-story/1b9ccca3eea6bd5f927656101c37e1c9 |archive-date=2017-09-12 |access-date=2017-09-19 |work=Fox Sports}}</ref>

===2018===
In 2018, Vaughan was named in The NSW Blues squad for The [[2018 State of Origin series]]. Vaughan also had the honour of being the 1000th player to pull on a Blues jersey.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walsh |first=Dan |date=2018-05-31 |title=NSW Blues Origin rookie Paul Vaughan's life support scare |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/05/31/nsw-blues-origin-rookie-paul-vaughans-life-support-scare/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180612162111/https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/05/31/nsw-blues-origin-rookie-paul-vaughans-life-support-scare/ |archive-date=2018-06-12 |access-date=2018-07-29 |publisher=National Rugby League}}</ref> On 5 June, Vaughan told the ''Sydney Morning Herald'' that "The best thing I did in my career was to leave Canberra and go to St George Illawarra."<ref>{{Cite news |last=Helmers |first=Caden |url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/leaving-canberra-was-the-best-thing-paul-vaughan-has-done-20180531-p4zilu.html |title=Leaving Canberra was 'the best thing' Paul Vaughan has done |date=2018-06-05 |work=The Sydney Morning Herald |access-date=2018-07-29 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180605235321/https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/leaving-canberra-was-the-best-thing-paul-vaughan-has-done-20180531-p4zilu.html |archive-date=2018-06-05  |url-access=limited}}</ref> Vaughan went on to play in all 3 games of the 2018 State of Origin series as The Blues won the shield 2–1.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Walter |first=Brad |date=2018-07-03 |title=Stat of Origin: Dragons quartet eye off rare NSW Origin-premiership double |publisher=National Rugby League |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/07/03/stat-of-origin-st-george-illawarra-dragons-quartet-eye-off-rare-nsw-origin-premiership-double/ |url-status=live |access-date=2018-07-29 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180703120357/https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/07/03/stat-of-origin-st-george-illawarra-dragons-quartet-eye-off-rare-nsw-origin-premiership-double/ |archive-date=2018-07-03}}</ref>

===2019===
After a good start to the [[2019 NRL season]], Vaughan was selected to play for New South Wales in the [[2019 State of Origin series]].  Vaughan played in all 3 games and scored a try in the deciding match which was played at [[ANZ Stadium]]. The Blues won 26-20 courtesy of a last minute try to [[James Tedesco]]. New South Wales would go on to win the series 2-1 and retain the shield for the first time since 2005.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Johnson |first=Paul |date=2019-07-10 |title=Emotional Blues legend Wayne Pearce opens up on his son Mitchell's battle |publisher=News.com.au |url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/origin/emotional-blues-legend-wayne-pearce-opens-up-on-his-son-mitchells-battle/news-story/0bdd173d0bf51730d60783a379922735/ |url-status=live |access-date=2019-07-11 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190711075307/https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/origin/emotional-blues-legend-wayne-pearce-opens-up-on-his-son-mitchells-battle/news-story/0bdd173d0bf51730d60783a379922735/ |archive-date=2019-07-11}}</ref>

Vaughan made a total of 23 appearances for St George Illawarra in the 2019 season as they endured one of their worst ever seasons, finishing 15th on the table above the last placed Gold Coast. St George Illawarra had gone into the season as one of the clubs expected to make the finals and challenge for the premiership.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Chisholm |first=Ed |date=2019-09-02 |title=Paul McGregor facing the axe as St. George Illawarra Dragons coach following horror season |work=Sporting News |url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/paul-mcgregor-facing-the-axe-as-st-george-illawarra-dragons-coach-after-horror-season/1m4wnmg28k3j1mhmkd7qlvj2x/ |url-status=live |access-date=2019-09-13 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190905113456/https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/paul-mcgregor-facing-the-axe-as-st-george-illawarra-dragons-coach-after-horror-season/1m4wnmg28k3j1mhmkd7qlvj2x/ |archive-date=2019-09-05}}</ref>

On 30 September, Vaughan was named as prop in the Prime Minister's XIII side. On 7 October, Vaughan was named in the Australian side for the upcoming [[2019 Oceania Cup (rugby league)|Oceania Cup]] fixtures.

===2020===
On 6 August, Vaughan was stood down by the club and fined $10,000 after he was caught breaking the NRL's strict bubble restrictions by visiting an Illawarra cafe.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Chisholm |first=Ed |date=2020-08-06 |title=Dragons star Paul Vaughan expected to be stood down over reported NRL biosecurity breach |url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/dragons-star-paul-vaughan-expected-to-be-stood-down-over-reported-nrl-biosecurity-breach/goac2zb5u1f3107ab94popx8b/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210704220618/https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/dragons-star-paul-vaughan-expected-to-be-stood-down-over-reported-nrl-biosecurity-breach/goac2zb5u1f3107ab94popx8b/ |archive-date=2021-07-04 |access-date=2021-07-04 |website=Sporting News}}</ref>

Vaughan played 15 games for St. George Illawarra in the [[2020 NRL season]] as the club finished 12th. Following the conclusion of the season, Vaughan was not selected for the [[2020 State of Origin series]].<ref>{{Cite news |last=O'Loughlin |first=Liam |date=2020-10-26 |title=NRL 2020 Season Review: How will your side fare next year? |work=Sporting News |url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/nrl-2020-season-review-rugby-league-dragons-broncos-titans-tigers-knights-sharks-roosters-eels-rabbitohs-raiders-panthers-storm/k79lmelm2xgj1ffsf8xh7phob/ |url-status=live |access-date=2020-11-07 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201101065611/https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/nrl-2020-season-review-rugby-league-dragons-broncos-titans-tigers-knights-sharks-roosters-eels-rabbitohs-raiders-panthers-storm/k79lmelm2xgj1ffsf8xh7phob/ |archive-date=2020-11-01}}</ref>

===2021===
On 4 July, Vaughan was placed under investigation by the NRL after it was alleged he hosted a house party along with eleven of his teammates which went against the league's [[COVID-19 pandemic in Australia|COVID-19]] biosecurity protocols, as well as a breach of the [[COVID-19 pandemic in New South Wales#2021|NSW stay-at-home orders]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Hooper |first=James |date=2021-07-04 |title=Named: The Dragons players at Covid party endangering the entire NRL |url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/dragons/nrl-2021-covid19-breach-paul-vaughan-st-george-illawarra-dragons-house-party/news-story/b1c41f985e969ad54cb3e64aa89d3b76/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210704221418/https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/dragons/nrl-2021-covid19-breach-paul-vaughan-st-george-illawarra-dragons-house-party/news-story/b1c41f985e969ad54cb3e64aa89d3b76/ |archive-date=2021-07-04 |access-date=2021-07-04 |work=Fox Sports|location=Australia}}</ref> The following day, Vaughan was suspended for eight matches and fined $50,000 over the incident. It was also alleged that Vaughan withheld information from the NRL and NSW Police.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-07-05 |title=Dragons hit with $305,000 in fines, Vaughan banned eight weeks |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/07/05/dragons-hit-with-$305000-in-fines-vaughan-banned-eight-weeks/ |access-date=2021-07-06 |publisher=[[National Rugby League]]}}</ref> On 6 July, the St. George Illawarra board approved the termination of Vaughan's contract effective immediately.<ref>{{Cite web |title=BREAKING: $800k Dragons star Vaughan sacked 'immediately' over Covid party and lies |url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/breaking-800k-dragons-star-vaughan-sacked-immediately-over-covid-party/news-story/e24c16a9ee8cb66081d9938de06f6f1b/ |access-date=2021-07-06 |work=Fox Sports|date=6 July 2021 |location=Australia}}</ref> The illegal house party was one of three behavioural strikes against Vaughan.<ref>{{Cite news |last1=Chammas |first1=Michael |last2=Proszenko |first2=Adrian |date=2021-07-06 |title=Sexting scandal was first strike that led to Vaughan sacking |work=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]] |url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/sexting-scandal-was-first-strike-that-led-to-vaughan-sacking-over-house-party-20210706-p587cl.html |url-status=live |access-date=2021-07-06 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210706075028/https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/sexting-scandal-was-first-strike-that-led-to-vaughan-sacking-over-house-party-20210706-p587cl.html |archive-date=2021-07-06}}</ref>
On 28 July, Vaughan signed a contract with the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] for the 2022 season.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2021-07-28|title=Vaughan signs with Bulldogs in bid to resurrect NRL career|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/07/28/vaughan-signs-with-bulldogs-in-bid-to-resurrect-nrl-career/|access-date=2021-07-28|publisher=National Rugby League|language=en}}</ref>

===2022===
In round 1 of the [[2022 NRL season]], Vaughan made his club debut for Canterbury in their 6–4 victory against the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] at [[Queensland Country Bank Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.msn.com/en-au/sport/other/nrl-scorecentre-parramatta-eels-vs-gold-coast-titans-north-queensland-cowboys-vs-canterbury-bulldogs-live-scores-stats-and-results/ar-AAUZI2h?ocid=BingNewsSearch/|title=Canterbury Bulldogs edge North Queensland Cowboys in Townsville, Parramatta Eels beat Gold Coast Titans|publisher=MSN}}</ref> Following the resignation of coach [[Trent Barrett]], Vaughan was criticised by journalists for attacking the media despite explicit instructions from general manager [[Phil Gould (rugby league)|Phil Gould]] not to speak to the media.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Webster |first=Andrew |date=2022-05-17 |title=Why Paul Vaughan is the type of footballer the Bulldogs don't need |url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/why-paul-vaughan-is-the-type-of-footballer-the-bulldogs-don-t-need-20220517-p5am33.html |access-date=2022-05-29 |website=The Sydney Morning Herald |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Weidler |first=Danny |date=2022-05-21 |title=Barrett knew axe could fall if Dogs lost to Knights |url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/barrett-knew-axe-could-fall-if-dogs-lost-to-knights-20220521-p5ancq.html |access-date=2022-05-29 |website=The Sydney Morning Herald |language=en}}</ref>
On 22 June, Vaughan signed a two-year deal to join English side [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] starting in 2023. In round 20, Vaughan played his 200th first grade game in Canterbury's victory over [[Newcastle Knights|Newcastle]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/warrington-wolves-paul-vaughan-transfer-24294719/|title=Warrington continue recruitment drive as Wolves sign experienced NRL prop Paul Vaughan on two-year deal|website=examinerlive.co.uk}}</ref>

===2023===
Vaughan made his club debut for Warrington in round 1 of the 2023 Super League season as the side defeated [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] 42–10.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/23327596.warrington-wolves-42-leeds-rhinos-10-happened/|title=Warrington Wolves 42 Leeds Rhinos 10, as it happened|website=warringtonguardian.co.uk|date=16 February 2023 }}</ref>
In round 24, Vaughan scored two tries for Warrington in their 66–12 victory over [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66661853|title=Super League: Warrington Wolves 66-12 Castleford Tigers – Wire demolish struggling Tigers|publisher=BBC Sport}}</ref>

During Warrington's loss to St Helens in round 26, Vaughan was placed on report for trying to move an injured [[Sione Mata'utia]] during the clubs 18–6 defeat. Vaughan was later suspended for four matches over the incident which ended his season. Vaughan was also fined £500.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ex NRL star faces monster ban after 'gee up' charge |url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/news-2023-paul-vaughan-super-league-tackle-video-suspension-sione-matautia/da60cc81-f8e0-4538-b310-21a5dc0c1f9f |access-date=2023-09-21 |website=wwos.nine.com.au |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66860845|title=Paul Vaughan: Warrington Wolves forward suspended for four games by tribunal|publisher=BBC Sport}}</ref>

===2024===
On 8 June 2024, Vaughan played in Warrington's [[2024 Challenge Cup final]] loss against Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c2jj1x7m71zo|title=Wigan beat Warrington to win Challenge Cup|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
Vaughan played 24 games for Warrington in the [[2024 Super League season]] as the club reached the semi-final before losing to Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/24654682.warrington-wolves-2024-season-review-february-march/|title=Warrington Wolves 2024 season review, February and March|website=www.warringtonguardian.co.uk}}</ref>

===2025===
On 7 June, Vaughan played in Warrington's 8-6 Challenge Cup final loss against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn0gnqllpxlo|title=In pictures: Hull KR Challenge Cup victory parade|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 25 July, Vaughan signed a contract to join RFL Championship side [[York Knights|York RLFC]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/64039/paul-vaughan-to-join-york-knights-in-2026/|title=Paul Vaughan to join York Knights in 2026|website=www.rugby-league.com}}</ref>
Vaughan played 24 games for Warrington in the 2025 Super League season as they missed the playoffs finishing 8th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn0gnqllpxlo|title=In pictures: Hull KR Challenge Cup victory parade|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
